title: Combinatorics, Conditional Probability, and Simulation · Error Forensics
summary: Analyze staffing reliability, screening results, and queue risks. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a simulated operational risk model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose multiplication, permutations, combinations, or inclusion-exclusion from event structure.
Update probabilities with conditional information and Bayes reasoning.
Test independence and distinguish it from mutually exclusive events.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Monte Carlo evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Monte Carlo assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Design reproducible simulations and quantify Monte Carlo sampling error. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: math intermediate probability combinatorics. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Monte Carlo, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a simulated operational risk model.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: One large simulation produces an exact probability. This fails the Monte Carlo contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: ASA GAISE Reports (GAISE II and College revision materials) — Controls simulation-first statistical reasoning and interpretation.

content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design reproducible simulations and quantify Monte Carlo sampling error.

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.instruction: An outcome pays $25 with probability 0.25 and $0 otherwise. Calculate its expected value.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Weight each outcome by its probability: 25 × 0.25 + 0 × 0.75.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Weight each outcome by its probability: 25 × 0.25 + 0 × 0.75.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.01 of the exact value and includes $ or dollars.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Monte Carlo applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design reproducible simulations and quantify Monte Carlo sampling error.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Counting Design to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Counting Design.

checks.description: The calculated quantity is accurate to the stated tolerance and carries its requested unit.
checks.failureMessage: Recalculate from the documented model, check the scale, and include the requested unit.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using design and reproducible plus specific case evidence.
